Attending site to check Hydrostatic wetwell LIT2 (W11654153). Found hydrostatic probe goes to 22mA (out of range high) when power is cycled. Will plan to replace probe in the next few weeks. ROC was notified before and after maintenance activities.

On site responding to Generator fault & LIT 3/4 OOR
-Upon arrival, there was no power at the facility but the ATS panel was indicating we were connected to utility power with 600 V on each phase. Switched power to generator power but still the same issue. Although I was able to run a pump in local.
-Appears there was a power blip which briefly caused the generator to start. The generator breaker tripped, along with MCC "Panel AA". The tripped MCC Panel AA was the culprit- once breaker was reset, facility power resumed and facility was operating normally.
-It was found that the "duct heater"(1, 3, & 5) breaker in "distribution panel AA", was tripped and resetting this caused it to immediately trip again, subsequently tripping MCC "Panel AA". Work order for property services to address this issue will be created. 
-WWEL_LIT2 (wet well 1 hydrostatic level) began reading OOR once the other issue resolved. Instrumentation will respond to site to correct the issue.

LDTEXT:
Onsite to troubleshoot erratic diesel LIT reading
Informed ROC
Found that diesel LIT reading is noisy when RSP#3 is running. RSP#3 VFD speed feedback is connected on same analog input module as diesel LIT, just one channel before. Suspect some type of electrical interference between the two signals. Moved diesel analog signal (I10905) to same analog input channel number but on the next slot (I11005). Asked PCS to make the necessary changes in the PAC program. Monitored for a while. Seems better but will need to verify over the next couple of days
Made note on PAC drawings indicating the change
Informed ROC, armed building and left site

VNPSCD - Parth P finished his work on the pre-injection chlorine analyzer. Placed the chemical system in auto mode and monitored. Chemical system activated and within five minutes the combined chlorine residual triggered a hihi alarm. Parth P collecting a grab sample.

01:42 - VNPSCD - Grab sample was below the hihi alarm setpoint. When the chemical system activates the post injection combined reading will likely hit the same value. Contacted Jerry Z who directed to try again. Parth P standing by with a grab sample to determine if the reading is accurate.
01:50 - VNPSCD - Alarms returned on second attempt to activate chemical system. Parth P collected a grab sample and confirmed that the post injection analyzer is reading accurately. Called Jerry Z to inform that the system is not operating as expected. Jerry Z informed that the combined chlorine average is what controls the dosing setpoints and this analyzer is currently reading low. Jerry stated that if the twenty minute average analyzer reading does not update then PCS should be contacted.
02:13 - VNPSCD - The twenty minute average combined chlorine reading has not updated after forty minutes. Contacted Chris S who walked through the process of completing a combined chlorine average reading reset. Completed the combined chlorine average reading reset at the request of Chris S and the combined chlorine average value matched the current pre-injection combined chlorine reading. Returned the chemical system to auto and monitored the post injection values. The post injection readings stabilized within normal range indicating that dosing is normal and the issue has been resolved. Will monitor site closely. Created CMU5#W11664563 for Chris S.
